entries.reason: Use dedicated security review automations so PR velocity can scale without trading away safety
Automations post adds the runtime details: cloud sandboxes, verification, memory, and security review/incident-response examples.
Seal eval runtime so coding-agent scores measure problem solving, not answer retrieval
OpenAI's audit of SWE-bench Pro independently argues that benchmark quality needs agent-assisted review and reports that roughly 30% of tasks are broken.
Point your coding agents at one gateway instead of wiring every provider separately
Agent Stack explains the single-endpoint routing, failover, and usage tracking that the coding-agents setup page turns into concrete per-tool config.
Put agent bash and MCP processes inside an OS sandbox before you trust them
Containment write-up confirms the same 84% prompt reduction and broadens the pattern into environment-first security design.
Fork parallel workers from one cached session instead of starting fresh conversations
Anthropic's prompt-caching announcement independently documents up to 90% cost reduction and up to 85% lower latency when the same prompt prefix is reused across calls.
